utter_start_brief_cope.confirmation.text: Do you want to start the evaluation of coping strategies?

utter_explain_brief_cope.response[explan.text: The Brief-Cope is a self-report questionnaire consisting of 28 items designed to measure effective and ineffective ways of "coping" the state or a stressful life event
"Coping" itself is widely defined as an effort used to minimize stress that affects the experience of life

utter_types_of_coping_strategy.response_.text: The coping strategy for everyone can vary depending on each personality and the type and source of stress experienced. But in general psychologists distinguish the coping strategy into the two main categories, namely Emotion-Focused Coping and problem-focused coping

utter_types_of_coping_strategy.response[.text:
Emotion-focused Coping is one type of coping mechanism that focuses on your emotional condition. The trick is to reduce the negative emotional response from a condition caused by stress, for example feeling embarrassed, anxious, fearful, grieving, and frustrating. 
 This strategy is the only way that applies coping capabilities when stress sources cannot be controlled. But sometimes, the emotion-focused coping strategy actually makes you procrastinate to overcome the source of the problem. 
 This is less effective in resolving long-term problems. This strategy is also dangerous when the problem you face requires immediate handling.For example, early treatment of chronic diseases and financial problems In addition, emotion-focused coping strategy can also be destructive or actually add problems and stress.

Problem-focused coping is the best coping strategy. This method does not only focus on stress levels, but also at the root of the problem of the stress. This means that you can overcome stress as well as the trigger using this one coping mechanism. 
 You don't necessarily have to apply for this strategy for every situation. This method is actually the most effective to overcome stress sources from work or pressure while achieving a purpose. 
 For example, stress because of losing a relative that you care about or depression because of something that cannot be changed, you can only overcome the emotion-focused coping. In implementing this one coping mechanism, there are three important things that you must pay attention to, namely, problem solving abilities, good time management, and social support around you.

utter_how_to_choose_coping_strategy.resp.text: To determine which coping strategy or technique is suitable, you can first evaluate the brief-Cope and coincidences in the design to handle it
Firstly, have you ever heard of the Brief-Cope method?

utter_already_know_brief_cope.response.text: Good if you already know what the Brief-Cope method is. Because this method can help you to determine the suitable coping strategy for you

utter_start_brief_cope.response[confirm_.text: Good, evaluation for coping strategies will begin. This evaluation consists of 28 questions that you must answer. This evaluation will use the measuring method, the Brief-Cope. Keep in mind that honest answers will determine the accuracy of the evaluation results.
Well, you can anytime to evaluate the coping strategy. I will still save the results of your evaluation when there is a change.

utter_end_brief_cope.response[finish].text: The Brief-Cope Questionnaire has been completed, I will calculate it and you will be able to see the results below. Thank you, you have been strong to fill the questionnaire

utter_end_brief_cope.response[notes_1].text: To determine which coping strategy might suit you can see from the highest value of 3 coping strategies

utter_end_brief_cope.response[notes_2].text:
Notes: 
 For problem-focused, high scores indicates a coping strategy intended to change stress situations and show psychological strength, fortitude and practical approach to solving problems faced. 
 For emotion-focused, high scores indicate a coping strategy that aims to regulate emotions related to stress situations. High or low score has nothing to do with psychological health or poor health, but can be used to inform a broader formulation about someone's coping style. 
 For avoidants, high scores show physical or cognitive effort to break away from stressors, while for low is usually indicating adaptive coping (good coping).

utter_mental_disorder.when_to_consult_me.text: Consult with a doctor or psychologist if you experience the symptoms of the disorder. Don't let your condition get worse.

utter_mental_disorder.preparation_before.text:
Before consulting a psychologist or doctor, you first prioritize what to do: 
 - Make a list of symptoms that appear. 
 - Record the history of diseases ever and be experienced. Likewise with family medical history. 
 - Record all drugs, supplements, herbal medicines, or vitamins consumed. 
 - Record the questions you want to ask for a doctor. 
 - Ask your family or friend to accompany when consulting a doctor. They can provide moral and helpful support given the information delivered by the doctor.

utter_mental_disorder.during_the_consult.text:
In a session, a psychologist or a doctor will ask the following: 
 - What are the symptoms you feel? 
 - When did the first symptoms appear? 
 - Do you have a risk factor related to the disorder? 
 - Do you routinely consume certain drugs? 
 - Have you ever been looking for medical help? If yes, what are the treatment you have tried?

utter_mental_health_faq/the_mean_of_havi.text:
Mental diseases or disorders are health conditions that disturb the thinking, emotions, relationships, and everyday functions of someone. They are related to distress and reduced capacity to engage in ordinary activities of everyday life. 
 Mental illness also consists of its severity. Some are quite mild and just disturb some aspects of life, such as certain phobias. On the other hand, serious mental illness can lead to key functional disorders in everyday life. These include disorders such as severe depression, schizophrenia, and bipolar disorders, and may require that person to receive care at the hospital. 
 
 It is important to know that mental illness is a medical condition disrupts his/her relationship with character, intelligence, or willingness. Just like diabetes is a pancreatic disorder, mental illness is a medical condition caused by brain biology. Similar to  how someone will treat diabetes with drugs like insulin, mental illness can be treated with a combination of drugs and social support. This care is very effective, with 70-90 percent of individuals who receive treatment experiencing symptom reduction and improving quality of life. With the right handling, it is possible for people with mental disorders to be independent and successful

utter_mental_health_faq/causes_of_mental.text:
Mental illness does not discriminate. It can attack anyone regardless of sex, age, income, social status, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or background. Although mental illness can attack anyone, certain conditions are possible. More generally occur in different populations. For example, eating disorders tend to occur more often in women, while disorders such as disorders of concentration/hyperactivity more occur in children. Besides that all ages are vulnerable, but younger and older people are more vulnerable. 
 Mental illness usually attacks individuals at the top of their lives, with 75 percent of mental health conditions that are developing at the age of 24 years. This makes identification and treatment of mental disorders become very difficult, because normal personalities and behavioral changes in adolescence can cover symptoms of mental disorders (a mental health condition). Parents and caregivers must be aware of this fact, and pay attention to changes in mood, personality, personal habits, and withdrawal of their children. When this happens to children under 18 years, they are referred to as serious emotional disorders or serious emotional disturbance (SEDS)

utter_mental_health_faq/warning_signs_of.text:
Symptoms of mental health disorders vary depending on the type and severity of their conditions. The following is a list of common symptoms that might show mental health problems, especially when some symptoms are expressed at once: 
 

In adults: 
 • confused mind 
 • sadness or irritability which lasts for longer period of time 
 • extreme changes in moods (up and down) 
 • fear 
 • worries or excessive anxiety 
 • social withdrawal 
 • dramatic changes in eating habits or sleep 
 • strong angry feelings 
 • delusions or hallucinations 
 • increased inability to overcome problems and daily activities 
 • thinking about committing suicide 
 • numerous physical problems 
 • drug abuse and/or alcohol 
 

In older children and pre-teenagers: 
 • drug abuse and/or alcohol 
 • inability to overcome problems and daily activities 
 • changes in sleep habits and/or eat 
 • opposed authority, skipping school, stealing, or damaging property 
 • intense fear of gain weight 
 • negative mood that lasts a long time, often accompanied by bad appetite and thoughts about death 
 • an explosion of anger that occurs often 
 

In younger children:  n • changes in school performance 
 • bad performance even though there is a hard effort 
 • worries or excessive anxiety 
 • hyperactive 
 • constant nightmares 
 • continuous disobedience and/or aggressive behavior 
 • being angry often

utter_mental_health_faq/can_mental_illne.text: In the healing process of mental illnesses, early identification and treatment is very important. Based on the nature of the disease, there are various effective treatments available. For all types of care, it is important for people affected to be proactive and fully involved in their own recovery process. Many people with mental illnesses who are diagnosed and treated respond well, although some may experience the return of symptoms. Even in such cases, with careful monitoring and managing disturbances, it is still very possible to live a satisfying and productive life

utter_mental_health_faq/someone_who_have.text: Even though this application cannot replace professional advice, I suggest those who have symptoms to talk to their friends and family members and seek advice from mental health professionals. The faster the mental health conditions are identified and treated, the faster they can get to recovery. If you know someone who is having a problem, don't assume that the problem will be solved by itself. Tell them that you care about them, and that there is a choice of treatments available that will help them recover. Talk to mental health professionals or counselors if you think friends or family members experience symptoms of mental health. If the person you love has symptoms until the impact of mental disorders, they need to know that you support them, thus they mighwill ask for help to professional experts.

utter_mental_health_faq/how_to_find_ment.text: Feeling comfortable with professionals who work with you or your relatives is very important for the success of the treatment. Finding professionals that best suits your needs may require research.Start by looking for providers in your area.

utter_mental_health_faq/available_treatm.text: Just like there are various types of drugs for physical illness, different treatment options are available for individuals with mental illness. Treatment works differently for different people.Important to find what is most suitable for you or your other relatives.

utter_mental_health_faq/involved_in_trea.text:
The beginning of treatment is a big step for individuals and families and can feel overwhelmed. It is important to accompany and get involved in the treatment process as often as possible. There are some questions that you need to answer, which include: 
 • What is known about the causes of this particular disease? 
 • Is there another diagnosis where these symptoms usually occur? 
 • Do you usually include a physical or neurological examination? 
 • Is there an additional test or an exam that you will recommend now? 
 • Will you suggest an independent opinion of another psychiatrist at this time? 
 • What treatment programs are most helpful with this diagnosis? 
 • Does this program involve services from other specialists? If so, who will be responsible for coordinating this service? 
 • What do you see as a family role in this treatment program? 
 • How much access does family owned against individuals who provide care? 
 • What drugs are usually used for this diagnosis? 
 • How much experience do you have in caring for individuals with this disease? 
 • What can I do to help you in the treatment?

utter_mental_health_faq/mental_health_pr.text: There are many types of professionals in the field of mental health providers and services. They maybe confusing for those of us who have not yet known each of these professionals having various levels of education, training, and may have different fields of expertise. Find professionals who best suit your needs. It may need some research.

utter_mental_health_faq/where_else_to_ge.text:
Where you seek help will depend on the nature of the problem and/or symptoms and what is most suitable for you. Often, the best place to start is by talking to someone you trust about your worries, like family members, friends, pastors, health service providers, or other professionals. Having social support is very important in healing from mental illness, and you will be able to ask them for referrals or recommendations for trusted mental health practitioners. Find mental health resources in your area. 
 
 Maybe you can start from where you provide services to talk, listen, and help you do care. In addition, many people consider peer support to help your recovery. There are various organizations that offer support groups or support systems and some of these support groups are led by peers while others may be led by mental health professionals.

utter_mental_health/before_starting_new_.text:
The best source of information about medicine is a doctor who prescribes it. They should be able to answer questions asked by patients like this: 1. What should be done about the drug? 
 2. When will the drug start reacting, and how do I know that the drug is effective? 
 3. How do I take the medicine and how long do I have to take it? Food, drinks, other drugs, and what activities should be avoided while taking the drug? 
 4. What are the side effects of the drug and what should be done if the side effects occur? 
 5. What should I do if one dose of the drug is missed? 
 6. Is there written information available about the drug? 
 7. Are there other drugs that might be appropriate? If yes, why are you (doctor) preferring this drug? 
 8. How do you (doctor) monitor treatment and what symptoms indicate that the drug must be raised, lowered, or changed? 
 
 All drugs must be taken according to the instructions. Most drugs for mental illness do not work if taken irregularly, and additional doses can cause severe side effects and are sometimes dangerous. Many psychiatric drugs begin to have beneficial effects only after consuming for several weeks

utter_mental_health/is_after_taking_medi.text:
Frequently people stop taking medicine when they feel the symptoms are under control. Others may choose to stop taking medication because of the side effects, without realizing that most of the side effects can be managed effectively. Even though it might seem reasonable to stop taking medicine, the problem is, most often the symptoms will return. If you or your relatives are taking medicine, it is very important for you to work with your doctor before making decisions about any changes in your care. 
 The other problem being,  by stopping treatment, especially to stop it suddenly, you might experience symptoms of broken medicine. It can be very unpleasant if you and your doctor feel your drug trial is a good idea. It is necessary to slow down the dose of the drug so that these symptoms do not occur. 
 
 It is important for your doctor and pharmacist to work together to make sure your medication works safely and effectively. You have to talk to them about what you do and if there are side effects which makes you not want to continue treatment. They will work with you to develop a strategy to minimize these side effects, or will make a plan to switch to other more suitable treatments

utter_mental_health/find_the_right_thera.text: Various types of therapy are more effective based on the nature of the condition and/or symptoms of mental health and people who experience it (for example, children will benefit from the therapist who specializes in children's mental health). However, there are several types of care and therapy that can help.

utter_mental_health/learn_types_of_menta.text: Mental health conditions are often treated with treatment, therapy or combination of both. However, there are many types of treatments available including complementary & alternative treatments, self-help plans, and peer support. Personal care must be discussed by people with mental health and their team.

utter_mental_health/diffrent_types_of_me.text: There are many types of mental health professionals. Finding the right for you might need some research.

utter_mental_health/find_support_group.text: Many people consider peer support as a useful tool that can help their recovery. There are various organizations that offer support groups for consumers, family members and their friends. Some support groups are led by peers, while others may be led by a mental health professional.

utter_mental_health/find_inpatient_care.text: If you or someone you know is in a crisis, hospitalization care can help. Inpatient care can help people stabilize new treatments, adjust to new symptoms, or get the help they need

utter_mental_health/find_other_local_ser.text: Many services can be used to help you find mental health care in your community, especially using the internet. This information can help you find the right therapist, and allow you to better understand the choice of proper treatment and treatment process.

utter_mental_health/learn_more_about_cli.text: Sometimes, consumers of mental health services can consider participating in research studies when they do not experience improvement even though they have tried various treatments and care.Research Studies (also known as clinical trials) may involve the use of new drugs or new treatment approaches that security and effectiveness are being tested. Even though we support innovation in the field, consumers must be warned that there are risks associated with clinical trials, make sure you know it before registering.

utter_benefit_of_writing_gratitude.text:
Just giving information that by writing gratitude you get the following benefits: 
 1. Reducing the stress level: 
 Someone who is grateful have a tendency to keep oneself better, practice healthy living habits, and have good stress management skills. 
 2. Getting a new perspective: 
 By writing the Gratitude Journal, we can start observing and seeing patterns about what we think is important in life. This can help us appreciate every little thing we experience and face. 
 In addition, the way we see a situation depends on the perspective we use. When we choose to look for things to be grateful, our perspective in seeing something will change and we are able to realize things that have never been thought of before. 
 3. Give rise to self-awareness: 
 Knowing any important thing in life can help you to be more mindfull in deciding something. You can be someone who is wiser in making decisions because you understand what you really like. 
 This can help you sort things when you have to make a choice. You can be a person who is not easily washed away on the current by following what many people are followed by most people. 
 4. As a reminder in a difficult time: 
 Without the Gratitude Journal, you will have trouble remembering what good things you have experienced. Especially when in difficult times, anyone can easily dissolve in sadness and forget other things that can be grateful

utter_mindfullness_description.response_.text: This is one of the meditation techniques that are widely used by many people, moreover this technique is trendy among teenagers. The technique is known as Mindfulness.
Mindfulness is a meditation that trains someone to focus on the situation(state) around them. This Mindfullness meditation has various benefits for the body, physical health, and mental health.
Mindfullness meditation can be done by anyone without special requirements. This type of meditation can also be done anytime and anywhere, both at home, office, or meditation class.

Actually there are many simple ways to do Mindfullness and everyone has their respective preferences. I know 4 forms of Mindfullness exercises, such as: 
 1. Attention: 
 Try to spend a moment to pay attention to the environment using all the five senses owned. Starting from touch, voice, vision, smell, and taste. 
 For example when you eat your favorite food. Before eating, try smelling the aroma, feel, until it's really enjoyed. 
 2. Focus on present: 
 The next mindfulness exercise is to try to focus on what happens to your life right now. Try to pay attention and accept what is already in life. So you will find excitement and pleasure though simple. 
 3. Accept yourself: 
 Another form of mindfulness meditation that is good for the body is to accept oneself as it is. Treat yourself as a good friend figure. 
 4. Focus on breathing: 
 When you have negative thoughts, try to sit, take a deep breath and close your eyes. Focus on breathing in and out in the body. Even though your breathing exercises are for just one minute, still this can make you more calm. So negative thoughts can be appeased.

utter_mindfullness_end_description.respo.text: Hopefully, the information I gave can help you do this mindfulness exercise, the key is to be patient and continue to practice.

utter_menu_options_mood_journaling.respo.text: Mood journaling is the activity to record your emotions everyday through the journal. You can record any emotion that you feel on that day, whatever you think, and what caused them. Usually equipments that are needed to be prepared is only a notebook and pen or colored markers.

Recording the occurrence of everyday feelings have impressive changes. Many people do not expect if journaling can be useful for mental health. Journaling can also be done as therapy and relaxation when done routinely. Here are some benefits of journaling for mental health: 
 • Identify external and internal triggers that cause mood changes. 
 • Learn more about the influence of factors such as sleep, diet, and daily activities on moods. 
 • Help find patterns and understand mood changes. 
 • Help determine the type of treatment that can help improve mood.
On the application you use this feature, you can use it for your daily mood Journal.
